items.reason: User request captured verbatim with visual-reference constraint.
Ambiguities preserved as assumptions and open questions.
Core, feature-map, architecture, frontend, and backend knowledge files loaded.
All formal features map to live feature-map entries.
Required integrations, env vars, webhooks, and setup steps captured.
plan.json, decisions.json, and validation_report.json exist in the run root.
Plan is LOCKED and route inventory is fully covered by page specs.
Data flows, env vars, routes, and smoke checks are declared.
Zero-warning CI policy is defined in testing and devops artifacts.

constraints.reason: Every feature has a primary integration.
Required integration components are captured in integrations.json and planner artifacts.
All required env vars are listed.
Sanity webhook is declared in plan and backend plan.
CMS-backed marketing surfaces are planned with Sanity schemas and routes.
Service and area routes use slug-driven CMS content types.
Auth is not present and no local auth is introduced.
Frontend and backend responsibilities are separated in the plan.
Secrets are env-driven only.
Sanity webhook verification is planned.
Webhook route is planned as idempotent by payload and revalidation target.
Content ownership remains in the CMS and no app DB is introduced.
Named integrations, env vars, support tools, and event names resolve to knowledge artifacts.
Plan is emitted as LOCKED with no pending TODOs.
Boot-time env validation is declared.
No auth routes in scope; no violation introduced.
Public-only marketing route set is explicit.
Server-only env vars are not exposed in public client plans.
Singleton clients are declared for used integrations.
Each feature is attached to a data flow.
Zero-warning enforcement is declared in CI gates.
Devops plan includes deterministic env and startup readiness.
npm run dev readiness is supported by the planned Next.js stack and env bootstrap.
Planning stayed within fix-free planning mode and emitted artifacts only.
Plan and frontend spec artifacts reconcile route-for-route.
Frontend artifact bundle is present and referenced by the aggregate plan.
Testing plan uses real unit, integration, and E2E gates.
Execution acceptance expectations are captured for downstream execution.
Evidence-bearing planning artifacts were emitted for all declared surfaces.

checks.reason: Design tokens centralised in design-system.tokens.json.
All public marketing pages declare seven or more sections unless exempt.
Component specs declare required states.
Interactive states are declared across buttons, inputs, accordion, and cards.
Visible strings route through content-library.md and content.en-AU.json.
All declared motion patterns include reduced-motion fallbacks.
Page specs include required completeness sections.
Every dynamic section declares data source and query/call.
Micro motion declarations cite purpose.
Quote and contact forms declare fields, validation, and recovery states.
Single visual archetype selected.
No hover-only discovery is planned.
Interactive elements include keyboard interaction notes.
Focus-visible treatment is standardised.
Token pairings are planned for AA contrast.
Landmarks and heading outlines are declared on every page spec.
Form labels and error association are specified.
Meaningful hero and team imagery require alt text.
Reduced-motion coverage is explicit.
44px touch targets are standard for mobile actions.
Skip links are declared on all page specs.
ARIA discipline is documented for accordions, forms, and busy states.
All disclosures have tap parity.
Locale posture is declared as en-AU.

security_constraints.reason: Logging plan excludes PII and tokens.
No AI routes are in scope.
Server SDKs remain in backend plan only.
No file uploads are in scope.
No multi-tenant data layer is in scope.
No sensitive admin or billing operations are in scope.
Dependency audit is included in CI policy.

performance_constraints.reason: No large-table queries exist because no app DB is in scope.
Long-running work is limited to email send and webhook revalidation within small scope.
No AI routes are in scope.
All imagery is planned through next/image.
Cache strategy is declared per data source.
No high-frequency cached aggregate outside CMS reads is required.
No production SQL repository layer is in scope.
Bundle analysis is part of CI expectations.
No list endpoints return unbounded API payloads; CMS list pages are page-level reads.
Marketing-site middleware remains edge-compatible and lightweight.

data_constraints.reason: Single source of truth is the CMS for content.
No DB migrations are in scope.
No seed process is required without an app DB.
PII fields are declared for lead capture.
Schema duplication is avoided by using CMS as the content source of truth.
Backup strategy is declared via support stack and devops plan.

checks.reason: Critical journeys have E2E coverage.
Service layer is isolated and dependency-based in the backend plan.
CI blocks on lint, typecheck, tests, and build.
All named integrations resolve to YAML rule files.
Required skills resolve to knowledge/skills files.
lead.created exists in the outbound taxonomy.
Support tools resolve to support-tool YAML files.
Preset override for optional CMS activation is recorded.
No explicit compliance regime is violated by the selected integrations.

verdict_reason: The plumber marketing-site planning run is complete, route-complete, and aligned to the supplied hero reference within the canonical DOC/output root.
